title: Minimum Spanning Trees and Network Design · Proof Workshop
summary: Connect sites at minimum build cost while explaining redundancy limits. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a minimum-network design and sensitivity brief while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use safe-edge cut arguments to justify minimum-spanning-tree choices.
Run Kruskal with deterministic edge ordering and disjoint-set cycle prevention.
Run Prim with frontier priorities and stale-entry handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
